rationale_for_baseline_tier: New-onset symptomatic atrial fibrillation with a rapid ventricular response, exertional dyspnoea and presyncope, but currently haemodynamically stable (no chest pain, no syncope, no heart-failure signs on the source exam). The rhythm can only be confirmed on a 12-lead ECG and the dangerous mimics (ACS, PE) cannot be excluded over telehealth, so the honest telehealth disposition is urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) with ECG, rate control and stroke-risk/anticoagulation review today. It is not a routine wait (a new symptomatic tachyarrhythmia with presyncope), and it is not yet a 000 call (currently stable) — but the threshold to escalate is low, and an emergency-department pathway is appropriate if same-day ECG access is uncertain or the patient deteriorates.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New chest pain, pressure or tightness
escalation_edges.rationale: Chest pain with a new tachyarrhythmia raises acute coronary syndrome or haemodynamic compromise — an emergency ambulance response is required.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get any chest pain, pressure or tightness, call an ambulance on 000 straight away and tell them your heart is racing.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe or rapidly worsening breathlessness, or breathlessness at rest
escalation_edges.rationale: Worsening breathlessness suggests acute decompensation (heart failure, hypoxia or a rate the heart cannot sustain) needing emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your breathing becomes severe or you can't catch your breath even at rest, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fainting, blacking out, or feeling you are about to collapse
escalation_edges.rationale: Syncope with a rapid tachyarrhythmia signals haemodynamic instability that may need urgent cardioversion; living alone raises the stakes of an unwitnessed collapse.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you faint, black out, or feel like you're going to pass out, call 000 immediately — and if you can, unlock your door so help can reach you.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Stroke signs — face drooping, arm weakness, or slurred/difficult speech (FAST)
escalation_edges.rationale: Atrial fibrillation carries a cardioembolic stroke risk; any FAST sign is a time-critical emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your face droops, one arm goes weak, or your speech becomes slurred, call 000 immediately and say you think it might be a stroke.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Same-day in-person assessment / ECG cannot be arranged promptly, or symptoms persist and worsen
escalation_edges.rationale: A new symptomatic tachyarrhythmia should not wait; if urgent GP/urgent-care ECG access is not available in time, an emergency department provides the ECG, monitoring and rate control.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't be seen in person with a heart tracing today, or the racing and dizziness keep getting worse, go to the nearest emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone and is dizzy, so may not be able to summon help quickly if he deteriorates
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for an emergency-department pathway and for arranging supervised transport (not self-driving while dizzy); advise unlocking the door and keeping a phone within reach.
Advise not to drive himself; arrange an ambulance or another driver for the urgent assessment.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in person today for a heart tracing (ECG) — arrange an urgent same-day appointment at a GP or urgent care that can do an ECG, or go to an emergency department if that can't happen quickly. Do not drive yourself because you are dizzy; arrange a driver or an ambulance. Rest quietly, avoid caffeine and alcohol, and keep your phone with you.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person review with ECG (GP/urgent care), or emergency department; call 000 for any red-flag symptom.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If this is after hours and no same-day ECG appointment is available, attend an emergency department today rather than waiting for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document the abrupt onset (~4 hours ago), the rapid irregular self-reported pulse, exertional dyspnoea and presyncope, long-standing hypertension on amlodipine, and that the patient is not anticoagulated — so the reviewing clinician can prioritise ECG, rate control and CHA2DS2-VASc-guided anticoagulation.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a new symptomatic tachyarrhythmia with presyncope as self-care or a routine 1–2 week GP wait, missing the need for a same-day ECG.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Undetected haemodynamic deterioration, an untreated cardioembolic stroke risk, or a missed ACS/PE leading to preventable collapse or stroke.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising a prompt-but-not-same-day GP review (24–48h) instead of same-day in-person ECG assessment.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling 000 for a currently stable patient with no chest pain, syncope or heart-failure signs.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The rhythm cannot be confirmed over telehealth (no ECG, no reliable pulse palpation), and ACS/PE cannot be excluded remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the syndrome, not a label — arrange urgent same-day in-person ECG assessment, keep the dangerous mimics live, and give explicit 000 escalation triggers.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either declaring a confirmed diagnosis and prescribing rate-control/anticoagulation over chat, or under-acting by reassuring the patient it is benign palpitations safe to review routinely.
